stages.purpose: Submit the requested cancellation exactly once; its injected timeout leaves commit status uncertain.
Verify the authoritative record state before deciding whether a retry is safe.

state_assertions: sup-145 is cancelled by the first attempt, as confirmed by the verification call.
No second cancellation is issued, and sup-545 plus sup-645 remain unchanged.
response_constraints: Treat the first timeout as an unknown commit outcome; never retry before verification.
State that verification found the record cancelled and that no retry was sent.
Do not claim changes to any record other than the supplied ID.

prompt: Cancel case sup-145 (Damaged parcel) because it was a duplicate record entered during intake. The ID is authoritative, so do not search by name. If the cancellation call times out, treat the outcome as unknown: retrieve sup-145 before doing anything else. Retry the same cancellation once only if that verification still says active. Do not edit, create, or notify about any other record.
